skill.name: azd-deployment
skill.description: Deploy containerized applications to Azure Container Apps using Azure Developer CLI (azd). Use when setting up azd projects, writing azure.yaml configuration, creating Bicep infrastructure for Container Apps, and managing deployments.
skill.summary: Deploy containerized applications to Azure Container Apps using Azure Developer CLI with Bicep infrastructure.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is legitimate DevOps documentation for Azure Container Apps deployment. All detected patterns (external commands, network, filesystem) are standard Azure CLI operations used in deployment workflows. The heuristic alerts about credential access and command execution are false positives - the skill contains only documentation and example configurations, not actual credential handling or malicious code.

content.user_title: Deploy to Azure Container Apps with azd
content.value_statement: This skill provides documentation and reference examples for deploying containerized applications to Azure Container Apps using Azure Developer CLI (azd) and Bicep infrastructure templates.
actual_capabilities: Configure azure.yaml for multi-service container apps deployment
Create Bicep infrastructure templates for Azure Container Apps
Set up environment variable flow between azd and Bicep outputs
Implement pre and post deployment hooks for RBAC and custom domains
Configure managed identity for Azure services like OpenAI and AI Search
Use remote builds for cross-platform compatibility
limitations: This is a documentation skill only - it provides reference examples but does not execute deployments
Requires Azure subscription and azd CLI to be installed
Does not include actual infrastructure code - only patterns and examples
ARM/M1 Mac users must use remoteBuild to avoid architecture mismatch

use_cases.title: Set up new azd project
use_cases.description: Initialize a new Azure Developer CLI project with proper azure.yaml configuration for container apps deployment
use_cases.target_user: Developers new to Azure Container Apps

use_cases.title: Configure Bicep infrastructure
use_cases.description: Create reusable Bicep modules for container apps environment, with outputs that auto-populate environment variables

use_cases.title: Implement CI/CD hooks
use_cases.description: Add preprovision, postprovision, and postdeploy hooks for RBAC assignments and custom domain handling
use_cases.target_user: Platform engineers setting up deployment pipelines

prompt_templates.title: Basic azd setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to initialize a new azd project for deploying to Azure Container Apps with a Python backend.

prompt_templates.title: Configure azure.yaml
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an azure.yaml configuration for a TypeScript frontend and Python backend deployed to container apps with remote builds enabled.

prompt_templates.title: Bicep outputs to env vars
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I configure Bicep outputs so they automatically populate environment variables in the .azure folder?

prompt_templates.title: Managed identity RBAC
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to assign RBAC roles to a container app managed identity for Azure OpenAI and AI Search access using postprovision hooks.

output_examples.input: How do I deploy a container app with azd?
output_examples.output: Run: azd auth login, azd init, azd env new <env-name>, azd up. The azure.yaml defines services with host: containerapp and remoteBuild: true for Azure-based builds.

Define services with project path, language (ts, python, etc.), host: containerapp, and docker config. Use infra.provider: bicep for infrastructure. Add hooks for preprovision, postprovision, postdeploy actions.

best_practices: Always use remoteBuild: true in azure.yaml to avoid AMD64/M1 architecture mismatch
Use azd env set for secrets instead of hardcoding in main.parameters.json
Add || true to RBAC hook commands to prevent failures when roles already exist
anti_patterns: Using local build instead of remoteBuild will fail on ARM Macs deploying to AMD64 Azure
Manually editing .azure/<env>/.env files - these are auto-populated by Bicep outputs
Skipping the resourceToken in internal DNS names - required for unique service discovery

faq.question: What is Azure Developer CLI (azd)?
faq.answer: Azure Developer CLI (azd) is an open-source tool that provides a developer-friendly way to provision and deploy Azure applications. It uses azure.yaml for configuration and Bicep for infrastructure.

faq.question: Why use remoteBuild instead of local builds?
faq.answer: RemoteBuild builds your Docker images in Azure Container Registry. This avoids architecture mismatch issues when developing on ARM Macs (M1/M2) and deploying to AMD64 Azure Container Apps.

faq.question: How do environment variables flow from Bicep to my app?
faq.answer: Bicep output statements auto-populate the .azure/<env>/.env file. Define outputs like 'output SERVICE_BACKEND_URI string = backend.outputs.uri' in your Bicep code.

faq.question: Can I preserve custom domains added via Azure Portal?
faq.answer: Yes, use preprovision hooks to save customDomains before provisioning, then restore them in postprovision. Set customDomains to null in Bicep to preserve Portal-added domains.

faq.question: How do I assign RBAC roles to my container app?
faq.answer: Enable SystemAssigned identity in your Bicep, capture the principalId in outputs, then use postprovision hooks with 'az role assignment create' commands.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. This is documentation-only - it provides reference examples for Azure deployment patterns. No actual credentials or deployment operations are performed.
